provenance.source_corpus: Ecological Factors Affecting White Pine, Red Oak, Bitternut Hickory and Black Walnut Underplanting Success in a Northern Temperate Post-Agricultural Forest

source_spans.text: The establishment success of white pine and red oak was measured with and without tree shelters (to protect from deer).
After 6 years, the survival and total height of unprotected oak was 29% and 44.3 cm vs. 80.5% and 138.5 cm for protected oak.
White pine was less affected by browsing (survival of 79.5 and 93.5%; height of 138.5 and 217.9 cm for unprotected vs. protected pine).
After 6 years, the survival and total height of unprotected oak was 29% and 44.3 cm vs. 80.5% and 138.5 cm for protected oak.
White pine was less affected by browsing (survival of 79.5 and 93.5%; height of 138.5 and 217.9 cm for unprotected vs. protected pine).
height of all hardwoods was higher in the white ash stands
Height of white pine was higher in the grey birch stands
height of all hardwoods was higher in the white ash stands
which had better soil drainage
higher fertility
understory dominated by Rubus species
highest canopy openness
total basal area
basal area of grey birch
soil CEC
soil NO3 supply rate
Gramineae cover
total height of bitternut hickory
total height of black walnut
